question.text: In which year were currency futures first introduced, and by which division of CME?

answer.text: 1972, by the International Monetary Market (IMM) division of CME
1973, by the Chicago Board Options Exchange (CBOE)
1975, by the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT)
1982, by the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) directly

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In 1972, the International Monetary Market (IMM), a division of the Chicago Mercantile Exchange, introduced the first currency futures contracts. This was a major milestone in financial derivatives. **The Trap:** 1973 was when CBOE introduced listed options (not currency futures). 1975 saw T-bill futures on CBOT. 1982 saw Eurodollar futures and first stock index futures. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series I (Currency Derivatives) also tests this historical fact. The progression: 1972 currency futures -> 1975 T-bill futures -> 1977 T-bond futures -> 1982 stock index futures. **Key Takeaway:** IMM (CME) 1972 = first currency futures. This opened the door to financial derivatives beyond commodities.
